title: Squawk
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:SquawkHS.png]] |name=Squawk |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}}''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] ***[[612th Attack Battalion]]{{SideshowCite|set=Militaries of Star Wars|packtype=Sixth Scale Figure|pack=Commander Ganch (612th Attack Battalion)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/Commander_Ganch_Sideshow_Collectibles_Celebration_VI.asp}} |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Let's see those muscles the Kaminoans worked so hard to make.''"
"''I'd rather use them to crunch droids than block rivers, sir.|Ganch and Squawk|}} '''Squawk''' was a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] who served in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. While stationed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]], Squawk served under [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Ganch]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Bolla Ropal/Legends|Bolla Ropal]]. Working with Ganch and Ropal's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] learner, [[Tyzen Xebec]], Squawk was given the duty of raising a [[Dam/Legends|dam]] wall at Ropal's behest. The work interested Squawk little, as he felt his talents were better put to use in a combat situation. His constant quips earned him Ganch's ire, and a reprimand. While working, the Republic [[Troop/Legends|troops]] were [[Battle of Devaron/Legends|attacked]], and Ropal was [[Death/Legends|killed]] shortly afterward. Squawk remained assigned to Xebec when the Padawan was given a new master, [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Keelyvine Reus]]. Squawk joined Xebec and Ganch as part of a team dispatched to the surface of the [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]], in order to properly assess the world's defenses against a potential [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] attack. During [[Project Instinction]], an [[Battle of Ukio|enemy initiative]] that attacked the neural processes of living beings, Squawk started attacking harmless [[gigaun]]s, which brought him into direct and open conflict with Commander Ganch. Project Instinction was later foiled by Xebec. ==Biography== ===Attack on Devaron=== {{Quote|Watch that lip, Squawk.|Commander Ganch|}} [[File:Squawk_workin_hard.png|thumb|left|200px|Squawk hard at work on Devaron]] Squawk was the taken name of a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]], [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. With the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|onset]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], he served in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. He was assigned to [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Ganch]]'s group, who in turn served under [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Bolla Ropal/Legends|Bolla Ropal]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Tyzen Xebec]]. Ropal's unit was dispatched to [[Devaron/Legends|Devaron]] to establish a Republic presence, and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master wished to aid the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]] with good-will projects during the occupation. To that end, he issued that a [[Dam/Legends|dam]] be built, in an attempt to restore the ecological system of a once-barren valley. Squawk was one of the clones assigned to the task of damming the [[Water/Legends|river]], and had to go through the grueling task of erecting the dam wall. [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Ganch oversaw the process, and as he instructed that Squawk and his compatriots use their honed muscles to raise the dam, Squawk remarked that he would rather use them to crush enemy [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. Ganch heard the remark, and reprimanded Squawk for his transgression. Soon, Devaron endured a Confederate [[Battle of Devaron/Legends|attack]], and enemy forces swept toward Squawk's position. The [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|Low Altitude Assault Transport]] parked atop the dam wall was swiftly destroyed by the enemy, and through Xebec, the clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] were ordered to hold their position by Ropal. Although the Jedi Master was eventually [[Death/Legends|slain]],{{TCW|Cargo of Doom}} Squawk survived the engagement. He, along with Commander Ganch and Xebec, were transferred to the command of [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Keelyvine Reus]]. ===Mission to Ukio=== {{Quote|Ready for dirtside, boys. Hope you all brought something interesting to read.|Squawk|}} When Reus's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was dispatched to the [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]] to prevent a possible Confederate takeover, Squawk joined Ganch and Xebec as part of a team dispatched to the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] surface to inspect defense systems. While aboard an LAAT [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]] in a journey to the planet's surface, Squawk quipped that they would likely be spending more [[Time/Legends|time]] reading than anything, prompting another reprimand from Ganch. Xebec agreed with Squawk, as Ukio's defenses were understood to be generally sound. Ganch, however, was sure that the thorough inspection of the [[Ukian/Legends|Ukian]] defenses requested by [[General/Legends|General]] Reus would take up most of their time. While on Ukio, they accompanied Commander Ganch to the [[Ukian shield generator]], outside of which Squawk and his fellow clones waited. Eventually, the sky was blacked out by agents of the Confederacy, causing panic. The issue was alleviated by a brief opening of the [[Planetary shield/Legends|shield]] by the [[Ukian/Legends|Ukian]] [[Unidentified liege|liege]], which dispersed the [[Moorja/Legends|Moorjan]] soil being used to obscure the [[Ukio system's star|sun]]. [[File:Ganch_vs_Squawk.png|thumb|right|200px|Squawk and Ganch team up against Tyzen Xebec.]] Shortly after the cloud was dispersed and the skies were cleared, the gargantuan [[gigaun]]s began breaking off from their usual tracks, and began trampling crops. A herd of them began moving dangerously close to the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] complex, prompting Squawk to open fire on the defenseless creatures, hoping to dissuade them. When Commander Ganch discovered this, he ordered Squawk to hold his fire, but the [[Troop/Legends|trooper]] disobeyed, dryly asking if he could expect a triplicate-signed order once the gigauns had flattened he and his fellow clones. When Ganch reprimanded Squawk again, he remarked that his Commander had a soft spot for unintelligent [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentients]]. This angered Ganch, and he drew his weapon on Squawk as a response. The trooper dropped his weapon, and asked the Commander to do likewise, but Ganch by this point was uninterested. Suddenly, Xebec intervened, using his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to destroy Ganch's pistol, before demanding an explanation from the two clone troopers. However, Squawk and Ganch then turned on Xebec. The Commander, struggling against his new-found violent stupor, was unable to stop himself from violently striking and pursuing the Padawan, with intent to kill. Ganch was eventually subdued, and Xebec was able to locate the [[Project Instinction device|devices]] causing the irregular behavior in those on Ukio, including Squawk. The devices were part of a [[Battle of Ukio|Confederate initiative]] named [[Project Instinction]], spearheaded by the enemy tactician "[[Doctor (tactical droid)|Doctor]]." The Republic won the day, averting total disaster on Ukio initially, but the tumultuous proceeding had resulted in a massive crash in stock value for Ukio itself. [[Magistrate/Legends|Magistrate]] [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]] of the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]] was able to purchase a controlling interest in the planet, forcing the Republic to leave the planet to their enemies, rendering their entire defensive effort moot. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|One with a soft spot for dumb animals.|Squawk teases Ganch in spite of the latter telling him to cease fire|}} Squawk spoke his mind while raising the dam on Devaron, earning reprimand from his Commander, Ganch. He felt that he would rather be utilizing his skills and strength to combat Confederate battle droids, rather than "block rivers." He also suspected that his assignment on Ukio would be a boring one, and quipped to his fellow clone troopers that they would likely be spending more time reading than seeing any action. While under the influence of the Confederate Doctor's Project Instinction devices, Squawk resorted to attacking defenseless animals, and became outright insubordinate to his superior officer, Commander Ganch, even going so far as to insult his fellow clone. Like all clone troopers sourced from Jango Fett, Squawk stood 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] high. ==Equipment== Squawk sported standard [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I clone trooper armor]]. His customizations were featured on his helmet, on which he had painted a set of [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a jagged row of sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. He also donned painted spaulders. His weapon of choice was the [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15S]]. ==Behind the scenes== Squawk first appeared in the [[Star Wars comic books|webcomic]] ''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'', published in [[2009]], which was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. He was introduced in the first installment of the comic, wherein he was rendered by [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Squawk}} *''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'' {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Squawk |nl=Squawk |pt=Squawk }} [[Category:Clone troopers]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Members of the 612th Attack Battalion]]
